title: Sri Sengazhuneer Amman Temple
tagline: Sri Sengazhuneer Amman Temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

heroImage.alt: Sri Sengazhuneer Amman Temple, Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Sengazhuneer Amman Temple செங்கழுநீர் அம்மன் கோவில் is situated at Veerampattinam in the Union Territory of Puducherry, India.
overview.description: Sengazhuneer Amman Temple செங்கழுநீர் அம்மன் கோவில் is situated at Veerampattinam in the Union Territory of Puducherry, India. It’s known to be an oldest and powerful temple in Puducherry. The age of the temple is not known for centuries and could be easily about 1000 years old.

topPlaces.name: Veerampattinam
topPlaces.description: Veerampattinam is the largest coastal village in the union territory of Puducherry situated between Pondicherry and Cuddalore. The village is 7 km away from the Pondicherry city centre and frequent bus services are available from Pondicherry for almost once in every 10–15 minutes.

topPlaces.name: Arikamedu
topPlaces.description: Arikamedu is an archaeological site in Southern India, in Kakkayanthope, Ariyankuppam Commune, Puducherry. It is 4 kilometres (2.5 mi) from the capital, Pondicherry of the Indian territory of Puducherry.

topPlaces.name: Kakkayanthope
topPlaces.description: Kakkayanthope is a village in Ariyankuppam Commune in the Union Territory of Puducherry, India.

topPlaces.name: Ariyankuppam River
topPlaces.description: Ariyankuppam River is a distributary of Sankaraparani River. It branches off Sankaraparani near Thirukanchi.

topPlaces.name: Manavely
topPlaces.description: Manavely is a Census Town in Ariyankuppam Commune in the Union Territory of Puducherry, India.

topPlaces.name: Sankaraparani River
topPlaces.description: The Sankaraparani River (French: Fleuve Sankaraparani) is a river in Tamil Nadu state of southern India. It originates on the western slope of the Gingee Hills in Viluppuram District, and flows southeastwards to empty into the Bay of Bengal south of Pondicherry.

topPlaces.name: Kamaraj Nagar
topPlaces.description: Kamaraj Nagar is a residential area of Pondicherry Town in the Union Territory of Puducherry, India. It is surrounded by Chinna Subrayapillai Street in the east, Kazhini (Paddy Fields) in the south, Government Primary Health Center and Sollai Counder Street in the North and Church of Our Lady of Good Health in the West.

topPlaces.name: Ariyankuppam
topPlaces.description: Ariyankuppam (also known by its former name Ariancoupom) is a Town, Commune, Sub-Taluk, and Assembly Constituency in the Union Territory of Puducherry, India. The streets in Ariyankuppam are straight and in grid form, similar to Puducherry boulevard.

items.note: Veerampattinam is the largest coastal village in the union territory of Puducherry situated between Pondicherry and Cuddalore. The village is 7 km away from the Pondicherry city centre and frequent bus services are available from Pondicherry for almost once in every 10–15 minutes.

Arikamedu is an archaeological site in Southern India, in Kakkayanthope, Ariyankuppam Commune, Puducherry. It is 4 kilometres (2.5 mi) from the capital, Pondicherry of the Indian territory of Puducherry.
Kakkayanthope is a village in Ariyankuppam Commune in the Union Territory of Puducherry, India.
Ariyankuppam River is a distributary of Sankaraparani River. It branches off Sankaraparani near Thirukanchi.
Manavely is a Census Town in Ariyankuppam Commune in the Union Territory of Puducherry, India.

The Sankaraparani River (French: Fleuve Sankaraparani) is a river in Tamil Nadu state of southern India. It originates on the western slope of the Gingee Hills in Viluppuram District, and flows southeastwards to empty into the Bay of Bengal south of Pondicherry.

Kamaraj Nagar is a residential area of Pondicherry Town in the Union Territory of Puducherry, India. It is surrounded by Chinna Subrayapillai Street in the east, Kazhini (Paddy Fields) in the south, Government Primary Health Center and Sollai Counder Street in the North and Church of Our Lady of Good Health in the West.

Ariyankuppam (also known by its former name Ariancoupom) is a Town, Commune, Sub-Taluk, and Assembly Constituency in the Union Territory of Puducherry, India. The streets in Ariyankuppam are straight and in grid form, similar to Puducherry boulevard.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sri Sengazhuneer Amman Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sri Sengazhuneer Amman Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sri Sengazhuneer Amman Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chennai International Airport (128 km) and the nearest railway station is Chennai Central (141 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sri Sengazhuneer Amman Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sri Sengazhuneer Amman Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sri Sengazhuneer Amman Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Sri Sengazhuneer Amman Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sri Sengazhuneer Amman Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Veerampattinam, Arikamedu, Kakkayanthope, Ariyankuppam River, Manavely.
